findings.summary: PRE-EXISTING, not introduced by this diff: the non-blocking provenance-check step (publish.yml:213) lacks pipefail, so a transient npm view failure yields ATT='' which passes without warning signal — a narrow quiet-pass edge in a step that runs right after a content-asserted registry check; add set -o pipefail next time the file is touched

ruledOut: scope creep/drop: hunk-by-hunk diff match against claims (a)-(d); idempotent skip, assert retry, provenance check, canary, id-token: write byte-identical outside the two hunks
tokenless breakage: runner's .npmrc shape reproduced empirically with NODE_AUTH_TOKEN unset — npm view/ci work anonymously; npm 11.5.1 env-replace leaves undefined ${VAR} literal instead of throwing
quiet publish failure: OIDC trust mismatch fails loud (ENEEDAUTH/E404, bash -e); hypothetical quiet no-op caught by the content-asserted retry loop exhausting

provenance-automatic claim: verified against live docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers ('you don't need to add the --provenance flag'); conditions (trusted publishing + public repo + public package) all hold; 0.2.1 dist.attestations observed live
ADR-0051 conformance: 'built, NOT yet verified' disclosure present in workflow comment, commit message, and PR body; no overreach — v0.2.1 proof correctly attributed to the token path
YAML/colon hazard: all four workflow files parse with PyYAML; new step name uses '--' separators (PR #133 class absent)
protocols: no AI attribution; diff only removes a secret reference; NPM_TOKEN secret deletion observed (gh api secrets total_count 0)
first-publish-of-new-version regression: npm view exit codes observed (unpublished version -> exit 1 -> publish branch taken)
